dependencies: Third-party providers for administration, technology, transfer agency and other services
Third-party distribution and sales channels including broker-dealers, banks, investment advisers and financial intermediaries
Key personnel including portfolio managers and investment analysts
risks: Failure of third-party providers to fulfill obligations could adversely impact business
Cybersecurity incidents or breaches of third-party providers
Dependence on third-party distribution channels not controlled by the company
Consolidation in broker-dealer or banking industries could adversely impact revenues
Technology system failures or limitations could impede business management and growth
Loss of key personnel could cause loss of clients and affect operations

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation by SEC, FINRA, CFTC, DOL, USDT and other U.S. regulators
Subject to MiFID II regulations in Europe
AIFMD regulations for alternative investment fund managers in EU
UCITS regulations for investment funds in Luxembourg and Ireland
GDPR data protection requirements in EU
California Consumer Privacy Act and state privacy laws
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
Sanctions programs by USDT Office of Foreign Assets Control
Anti-money laundering and anti-terrorist financing regulations
DOL fiduciary rule changes and ERISA requirements
SEC rules on fund names, cybersecurity disclosure, climate-related disclosure
Potential designation as SIFI or G-SIFI with additional supervision
FTC Hart-Scott-Rodino Act premerger notification requirements
Derivatives regulations and restrictions on use by funds
Money market fund reform requirements
ESG and sustainable investing regulatory scrutiny
State escheatment laws for unclaimed property
Complex tax regimes in multiple jurisdictions
Banking and trust company regulations for fiduciary subsidiaries
Inducements and commission payment restrictions in EU, UK, Netherlands
Investment research financing rule changes under MiFID II
Form PF reporting requirements for private fund advisers
QPAM exemption requirements under DOL rules
Ongoing investigations by SEC, CFTC, DOJ into Western Asset Management trading activities

sanction_risks: Subject to sanctions programs administered by USDT Office of Foreign Assets Control
Subject to sanctions programs in non-U.S. jurisdictions where services offered
Geopolitical risks from Ukraine-Russia war may impact global economy and financial markets
Middle East wars and conflicts may expand globally and impact markets
Emerging market portfolios subject to risks from political developments, currency fluctuations, social instability, nationalization, asset confiscation
